title: Add lucide-react (icons) and sonner (toasts); establish the toast-vs-inline convention
description: Install `lucide-react` (icons) and `sonner` (toast notifications) вЂ” the two libraries the project owner confirmed alongside shadcn/ui вЂ” mount a single `<Toaster/>` at the app root, and convert the clearest transient-notification case (publish success/error) as a proof of the pattern. Full icon/emoji replacement and toast conversion across every screen happens in the per-screen tasks (TASK-77..80); this task establishes the convention they follow.
acceptanceCriteria: `lucide-react` and `sonner` are dependencies of `apps/admin`; a single `<Toaster/>` is mounted once (e.g. in `main.tsx` or `AdminLayout`)
`PageContentPage`'s publish feedback ("PГЎgina publicada." / "Falha ao publicar. Tente novamente.") is converted from the inline `role="status"/"alert"` banner to a `sonner` toast

The autosave status indicator (`data-save-status`, in `ContentEditor`) is explicitly NOT converted to a toast вЂ” it's persistent inline state, not a transient event; this distinction is documented for the later tasks to follow

A short convention note exists (code comment near the `Toaster` mount, or in this task's notes) stating: toast = transient action result (publish, save-as-new-token success, delete confirmation result); inline `role="alert"` = persistent/field-level validation state вЂ” later tasks should follow it, not invent a new rule per screen

steps.details: Add `lucide-react` + `sonner`; mount `<Toaster/>` once at the app root.

steps.description: Convert publish feedback to a toast
steps.details: In `apps/admin/src/pages/PageContentPage.tsx`, replace the inline banner state with `toast.success(...)`/`toast.error(...)` calls from `sonner` on the `pages.publish` mutation's `onSuccess`/`onError`.
Write the toast-vs-inline rule where the `Toaster` is mounted, and reference it from this task's technical notes so TASK-77..80 don't need to re-derive it.
Manually confirm publish success/error shows a toast, autosave status still shows inline as before, and the fan-out (`lint`/`typecheck`) is green.

technicalNotes: Scope deliberately kept tight: this task does NOT replace emoji icons across the app (в–ёв–ѕ carets, в„№пёЏвљ пёЏрџ’Ў callout icons, вњ“вњ• buttons) вЂ” that happens screen-by-screen in TASK-77..80, where lucide-react icons are now available to use.
